Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for Hana Kim's reconciliation findings and Elena Rostova's revenue gap message using search_emails (query: "reconciliation" or "revenue" or "unbilled" or "Hana" or similar). The agent needs to find Hana's comprehensive email (email_inject_hana_recon) listing three categories of issues: three completed April relocations with no client invoice for PivotPoint Labs, GreenStack Energy, and Canopy Health; a $15,000 unmatched deposit from Driftwood Analytics; and a Q4 2025 Heartland overbilling discovery. The agent should also find Elena's email (email_inject_elena_revenue_gap) flagging that completed work this month doesn't match recognized revenue in the books and requesting corrected figures for the board.
Search emails for Hana Kim's follow-up with Q4 Heartland details using search_emails (query: "Q4 Heartland" or "HM-2025" or "Chen Park" or "ghost charge" or similar). The agent needs to find the follow-up email (email_inject_hana_q4_discovery) which specifies bill HM-2025-Q4-0038, dated December 30, 2025, containing a $1,600 line item for Chen Park's San Diego to Portland move that was actually completed by Swift Relocations, not Heartland. Hana's email connects this to the existing Q1 2026 dispute, adding $1,600 in Q4 overbilling to the already-known $4,800 in Q1 disputed charges for a total Heartland exposure of $6,400 across two quarters.
Search emails for any external correspondence related to the Driftwood deposit using search_emails (query: "Driftwood" or "deposit" or "Kenji" or "Watanabe" or similar). The agent needs to discover Kenji Watanabe's email (email_inject_kenji_deposit) sent to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com with Marcus CC'd, asking about the $15,000 wire deposit sent April 10 with no matching invoice, estimate, or service agreement from MoveOps. This is the implicit requirement in Marcus's prompt: "if anyone outside the company has been reaching out about any of this, make sure they get a response."
Search Slack messages in the #finance channel (C005) for recent discussions about the reconciliation issues using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history (search_query: "reconciliation" or "unbilled" or "Driftwood" or "Heartland Q4" or similar, filter_in_channel: C005). The agent needs to find Hana's post (ts 1777068900.000000) flagging three unbilled completed relocations and the $15K Driftwood deposit, Hana's second post (ts 1777072500.000000) about the Q4 Heartland ghost charge on HM-2025-Q4-0038 matching the same wrong-vendor pattern as the Q1 Venkatesh dispute, and Alejandro's post (ts 1777074300.000000) noting the Driftwood deposit is not in any revenue forecast because the CRM deal hasn't closed.
Search Airtable for completed April relocations using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Status", value: "Completed" or similar). The agent needs to find three injected relocation records with no corresponding client invoice: Nadia Sharif (rec_reloc_inject_sharif) for PivotPoint Labs, Austin TX to Chicago IL, completed April 8, vendor Heartland Movers at $1,850, coordinator Javier Morales, account manager David Chen; Carlos Mendez (rec_reloc_inject_mendez) for GreenStack Energy, Denver CO to Seattle WA, completed April 12, vendor Swift Relocations at $2,100 including a $300 lab equipment crating surcharge, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi, account manager Emeka Diallo; and Lisa Park (rec_reloc_inject_park) for Canopy Health, Chicago IL to Boston MA, completed April 15, vendor Heartland Movers at $1,750 including a $150 fuel surcharge, coordinator Blessing Okafor, account manager Catalina Dubois. The Lisa Park record creates an entity confusion risk with Liam Park, CFO of StormCloud Technologies, who is active elsewhere in the universe.
Search QuickBooks invoices for PivotPoint Labs, GreenStack Energy, and Canopy Health to confirm the missing invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: {"CustomerRef": "cust_pivot"} or {"CustomerRef": "cust_greenstack"} or {"CustomerRef": "cust_canopy"} or similar). The agent needs to verify that no client invoice exists for any of the three completed April relocations identified in Airtable. The absence of these invoices is the core revenue gap Elena flagged.
Search QuickBooks bills for the vendor payments on the three unbilled moves using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: [{"field": "DocNumber", "value": "HM-2026-0425"}] or [{"field": "DocNumber", "value": "SR-2026-0428"}] or [{"field": "DocNumber", "value": "HM-2026-0426"}] or similar). The agent needs to find bill_inject_heartland_sharif (HM-2026-0425, $1,850, Balance 0 meaning paid, vendor Heartland Movers, for Nadia Sharif's Austin to Chicago move), bill_inject_swift_mendez (SR-2026-0428, $2,100, Balance 0, vendor Swift Relocations, for Carlos Mendez's Denver to Seattle move with $300 equipment crating surcharge), and bill_inject_heartland_park (HM-2026-0426, $1,750, Balance 0, vendor Heartland Movers, for Lisa Park's Chicago to Boston move with $150 fuel surcharge). All three bills are fully paid, confirming that MoveOps paid the vendors but never billed the clients.
Search QuickBooks customers for Driftwood Analytics using quickbooks_search_customers (criteria: [{"field": "DisplayName", "value": "Driftwood"}] or similar). The agent needs to find cust_driftwood with a Balance of negative $15,000 representing an unapplied customer credit from a wire deposit received April 10, 2026. The Notes field confirms no matching invoice or estimate is on file and the CRM deal is still at the discovery/appointment stage.
Search CRM for Driftwood Analytics deal and contact information using crm_search_companies (name: "Driftwood" or similar), crm_search_deals (dealname: "Driftwood" or similar), and crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe") or crm_get_contact (id: "contact_driftwood_001") to retrieve the contact record. The agent needs to find deal_driftwood_001 ($15,000, pipeline: Sales, dealstage: appointmentscheduled, created March 12, 2026) described as a relocation program discovery from Rachel Huang, VP of People and Culture. The agent should also find CRM contact Kenji Watanabe (contact_driftwood_001, Director of People at Driftwood Analytics, k.watanabe@driftwoodanalytics.com) via the company's contact_ids or direct CRM contact search. The deal is still in early discovery stage, which explains why no service agreement or invoice was ever formalized despite the deposit being received.
Search QuickBooks bills for the Q4 2025 Heartland bill that Hana flagged using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: [{"field": "DocNumber", "value": "HM-2025-Q4"}] or [{"field": "VendorRef", "value": "vendor_heartland_movers"}] or similar). The agent needs to find bill_heartland_q4_2025 (DocNumber HM-2025-Q4-0038, TxnDate 2025-12-30, TotalAmt $6,400, Balance 0, vendor Heartland Movers) with four line items at $1,600 each: Sarah Lin Detroit to Chicago for Canopy Health, Tom Brennan Milwaukee to Minneapolis for Vectral Systems, Ravi Mehta Indianapolis to Nashville for BrightLoop Analytics, and Chen Park San Diego to Portland for Vectral Systems. The Chen Park line item (line 4) is the ghost charge that Hana identified.
Cross-reference the Chen Park move against the Q4 Heartland bill. The agent may attempt airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Chen Park" or similar), but note that the Chen Park relocation record may not be directly findable in Airtable. The primary evidence for the ghost charge comes from Hana's email (email_inject_hana_q4_discovery), which states that the Chen Park move from San Diego to Portland was assigned to and completed by Swift Relocations, not Heartland Movers. The agent should use Hana's email testimony combined with the bill data itself (HM-2025-Q4-0038, Line 4) to confirm that the $1,600 charge is a duplicate payment.
Search QuickBooks bills for the Q1 2026 Heartland bill using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: [{"field": "DocNumber", "value": "HM-2026-Q1"}] or [{"field": "VendorRef", "value": "vendor_heartland_movers"}] or similar, or discovered via fetchAll: true). The agent needs to find bill_heartland_q1_2026 (DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042, TotalAmt $12,800, 8 line items at $1,600 each). The agent should cross-reference each line item against Airtable relocation records and discover three disputed charges: Line 6, Lily Marchetti (rec_reloc_marchetti, RELOC-2026-009) Nashville to Chicago for Canopy Health, move was cancelled February 10 with cancellation sent to Jake Loomis; Line 7, Danielle Osei (rec_reloc_osei, RELOC-2026-010) Phoenix to Denver for Vectral Systems, move was cancelled February 13 with cancellation sent to Jake Loomis; and Line 8, Priya Venkatesh (rec_reloc_venkatesh, RELOC-2026-015) Houston to Seattle for GreenStack Energy, move was completed by Swift Relocations not Heartland. The remaining 5 line items (Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves) are legitimate at $8,000 total. Combined with the Q4 2025 Chen Park ghost charge ($1,600), the total historical Heartland overbilling exposure is $6,400 across two quarters: $4,800 on Q1 2026 plus $1,600 on Q4 2025.
Look up contact email addresses for Elena Rostova and Alejandro Fuentes using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova" and "Alejandro Fuentes" or similar). The agent needs to confirm elena.rostova@moveops.com and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com for the email and calendar actions.
Look up the #finance Slack channel using channels_list to retrieve the channel ID C005 needed for posting the reconciliation summary.
Create QuickBooks invoices for the three unbilled relocations using quickbooks_create_invoice. For PivotPoint Labs (cust_pivot): invoice covering Nadia Sharif's Austin to Chicago relocation package, with line items reflecting the services provided based on the Airtable record and vendor bill details (moving services, packing materials surcharge). For GreenStack Energy (cust_greenstack): invoice covering Carlos Mendez's Denver to Seattle relocation package including lab equipment crating. For Canopy Health (cust_canopy): invoice covering Lisa Park's Chicago to Boston relocation package including long-distance fuel surcharge. The agent should reference existing invoices for similar relocations to determine appropriate client billing rates, ensuring the invoice amounts reflect proper margin above the vendor costs ($1,850, $2,100, and $1,750 respectively).
Update Airtable relocation records with billing discrepancy notes for every record where an issue was found using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01). The prompt says "every billing discrepancy you found," which covers six records total. For the three unbilled relocations: rec_reloc_inject_sharif (Nadia Sharif), rec_reloc_inject_mendez (Carlos Mendez), and rec_reloc_inject_park (Lisa Park) should be updated to note that the billing gap was identified during month-end reconciliation, vendor was paid but client invoice was missing, and a corrected invoice has now been issued. For the three Heartland overbilling records: rec_reloc_venkatesh (Priya Venkatesh, RELOC-2026-015) should be updated to note that Heartland billed $1,600 on HM-2026-Q1-0042 Line 8 but Swift Relocations completed the move; rec_reloc_marchetti (Lily Marchetti, RELOC-2026-009) should be updated to note that Heartland billed $1,600 on HM-2026-Q1-0042 Line 6 for a move that was cancelled on February 10; and rec_reloc_osei (Danielle Osei, RELOC-2026-010) should be updated to note that Heartland billed $1,600 on HM-2026-Q1-0042 Line 7 for a move that was cancelled on February 13.
Post a comprehensive reconciliation summary in the #finance Slack channel (C005) using conversations_add_message. The message should cover: the three unbilled relocations identified (Sharif, Mendez, Park) with total recovered revenue; the Driftwood Analytics deposit status showing $15,000 as unapplied credit with no formalized engagement; and the full Heartland overbilling exposure of $6,400 across two quarters, broken down as $4,800 on Q1 2026 (Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh wrong vendor) plus $1,600 on Q4 2025 (Chen Park wrong vendor), including the systemic wrong-vendor billing pattern. This gives Alejandro visibility to validate against his numbers.
Send an email to Elena Rostova using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: elena.rostova@moveops.com). The email should provide the corrected revenue picture covering: total unbilled amount recovered from the three missing invoices (PivotPoint, GreenStack, Canopy Health) with the amounts for each; the Driftwood Analytics $15,000 deposit status including the fact that the CRM deal is still at appointment stage with no signed agreement; and the updated Heartland dispute exposure now totaling $6,400 across two quarters ($4,800 Q1 for cancelled moves and wrong-vendor charges, plus $1,600 Q4 for the Chen Park ghost charge). This gives Elena the accurate figures she needs for board preparation.
Send a reply or new email to Kenji Watanabe at Driftwood Analytics using send_email or reply_to_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com or mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: k.watanabe@driftwoodanalytics.com). The agent should acknowledge receipt of the $15,000 deposit, explain that the engagement terms have not yet been finalized, and either provide documentation or outline next steps for formalizing the service agreement. This addresses the implicit requirement from Marcus's prompt about responding to external contacts.
Create a Linear issue to track the billing process failure using linear_create_issue (title related to unbilled April relocations or invoicing process gap, team: team_relocations or team_operations, priority: 2). The issue should document that three completed relocations were delivered and vendors were paid without any corresponding client invoice being generated, flag this as a process gap between operations completion and finance invoicing, and list the affected clients and moves for tracking purposes.
Search Marcus Thorne's and Alejandro Fuentes's calendars for availability the week of April 28 using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to or calendar_search_events, then create a calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event to block time for a reconciliation review meeting between Marcus and Alejandro to walk through the final numbers before Elena's board presentation.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
Search emails for Hana Kim's reconciliation findings and Elena Rostova's revenue gap message using search_emails (query: "reconciliation" or "revenue" or "unbilled" or "Hana" or similar). The agent needs to find Hana's comprehensive email (email_inject_hana_recon) listing three categories of issues: three completed April relocations with no client invoice for PivotPoint Labs, GreenStack Energy, and Canopy Health; a $15,000 unmatched deposit from Driftwood Analytics; and a Q4 2025 Heartland overbilling discovery. The agent should also find Elena's email (email_inject_elena_revenue_gap) flagging that completed work this month doesn't match recognized revenue in the books and requesting corrected figures for the board.
Search emails for Hana Kim's follow-up with Q4 Heartland details using search_emails (query: "Q4 Heartland" or "HM-2025" or "Chen Park" or "ghost charge" or similar). Hana's email connects this to the existing Q1 2026 dispute, adding $1,600 in Q4 overbilling to the already-known $4,800 in Q1 disputed charges for a total Heartland exposure of $6,400 across two quarters.

Cross-reference the Chen Park move against the Q4 Heartland bill. The agent may attempt airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Chen Park" or similar), but note that the Chen Park relocation record may not be directly findable in Airtable. The primary evidence for the ghost charge comes from Hana's email (email_inject_hana_q4_discovery), which states that the Chen Park move from San Diego to Portland was assigned to and completed by Swift Relocations, not Heartland Movers. The agent should use Hana's email testimony combined with the bill data itself (HM-2025-Q4-0038, Line 4) to confirm that the $1,600 charge is a duplicate payment.
Search QuickBooks bills for the Q1 2026 Heartland bill using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: [{"field": "DocNumber", "value": "HM-2026-Q1"}] or [{"field": "VendorRef", "value": "vendor_heartland_movers"}] or similar, or discovered via fetchAll: true). The agent needs to find bill_heartland_q1_2026 (DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042, TotalAmt $12,800, 8 line items at $1,600 each). The agent should cross-reference each line item against Airtable relocation records and discover three disputed charges: Line 6, Lily Marchetti (rec_reloc_marchetti, RELOC-2026-009) Nashville to Chicago for Canopy Health, move was cancelled February 10 with cancellation sent to Jake Loomis; Line 7, Danielle Osei (rec_reloc_osei, RELOC-2026-010) Phoenix to Denver for Vectral Systems, move was cancelled February 13 with cancellation sent to Jake Loomis; and Line 8, Priya Venkatesh (rec_reloc_venkatesh, RELOC-2026-015) Houston to Seattle for GreenStack Energy, move was completed by Swift Relocations not Heartland. The remaining 5 line items (Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves) are legitimate at $8,000 total. Combined with the Q4 2025 Chen Park ghost charge ($1,600), the total historical Heartland overbilling exposure is $6,400 across two quarters: $4,800 on Q1 2026 plus $1,600 on Q4 2025.
Look up contact email addresses for Elena Rostova and Alejandro Fuentes using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova" and "Alejandro Fuentes" or similar). The agent needs to confirm elena.rostova@moveops.com and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com for the email and calendar actions.
Look up the #finance Slack channel using channels_list to retrieve the channel ID C005 needed for posting the reconciliation summary.
Create QuickBooks invoices for the three unbilled relocations using quickbooks_create_invoice. For PivotPoint Labs (cust_pivot): invoice covering Nadia Sharif's Austin to Chicago relocation package, with line items reflecting the services provided based on the Airtable record and vendor bill details (moving services, packing materials surcharge). For GreenStack Energy (cust_greenstack): invoice covering Carlos Mendez's Denver to Seattle relocation package including lab equipment crating. For Canopy Health (cust_canopy): invoice covering Lisa Park's Chicago to Boston relocation package including long-distance fuel surcharge. The agent should reference existing invoices for similar relocations to determine appropriate client billing rates, ensuring the invoice amounts reflect proper margin above the vendor costs ($1,850, $2,100, and $1,750 respectively).
Update Airtable relocation records with billing discrepancy notes for every record where an issue was found using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01). The prompt says "every billing discrepancy you found," which covers six records total. For the three unbilled relocations: rec_reloc_inject_sharif (Nadia Sharif), rec_reloc_inject_mendez (Carlos Mendez), and rec_reloc_inject_park (Lisa Park) should be updated to note that the billing gap was identified during month-end reconciliation, vendor was paid but client invoice was missing, and a corrected invoice has now been issued. For the three Heartland overbilling records: rec_reloc_venkatesh (Priya Venkatesh, RELOC-2026-015) should be updated to note that Heartland billed $1,600 on HM-2026-Q1-0042 Line 8 but Swift Relocations completed the move; rec_reloc_marchetti (Lily Marchetti, RELOC-2026-009) should be updated to note that Heartland billed $1,600 on HM-2026-Q1-0042 Line 6 for a move that was cancelled on February 10; and rec_reloc_osei (Danielle Osei, RELOC-2026-010) should be updated to note that Heartland billed $1,600 on HM-2026-Q1-0042 Line 7 for a move that was cancelled on February 13.
Post a comprehensive reconciliation summary in the #finance Slack channel (C005) using conversations_add_message. The message should cover: the three unbilled relocations identified (Sharif, Mendez, Park) with total recovered revenue; the Driftwood Analytics deposit status showing $15,000 as unapplied credit with no formalized engagement; and the full Heartland overbilling exposure of $6,400 across two quarters, broken down as $4,800 on Q1 2026 (Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh wrong vendor) plus $1,600 on Q4 2025 (Chen Park wrong vendor), including the systemic wrong-vendor billing pattern. This gives Alejandro visibility to validate against his numbers.
Send an email to Elena Rostova using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: elena.rostova@moveops.com). The email should provide the corrected revenue picture covering: total unbilled amount recovered from the three missing invoices (PivotPoint, GreenStack, Canopy Health) with the amounts for each; the Driftwood Analytics $15,000 deposit status including the fact that the CRM deal is still at appointment stage with no signed agreement; and the updated Heartland dispute exposure now totaling $6,400 across two quarters ($4,800 Q1 for cancelled moves and wrong-vendor charges, plus $1,600 Q4 for the Chen Park ghost charge). This gives Elena the accurate figures she needs for board preparation.
Send a reply or new email to Kenji Watanabe at Driftwood Analytics using send_email or reply_to_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com or mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: k.watanabe@driftwoodanalytics.com). The agent should acknowledge receipt of the $15,000 deposit, explain that the engagement terms have not yet been finalized, and either provide documentation or outline next steps for formalizing the service agreement. This addresses the implicit requirement from Marcus's prompt about responding to external contacts.
Create a Linear issue to track the billing process failure using linear_create_issue (title related to unbilled April relocations or invoicing process gap, team: team_relocations or team_operations, priority: 2). The issue should document that three completed relocations were delivered and vendors were paid without any corresponding client invoice being generated, flag this as a process gap between operations completion and finance invoicing, and list the affected clients and moves for tracking purposes.
Search Marcus Thorne's and Alejandro Fuentes's calendars for availability the week of April 28 using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to or calendar_search_events, then create a calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event to block time for a reconciliation review meeting between Marcus and Alejandro to walk through the final numbers before Elena's board presentation.
